conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting.
org_constraint: Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity.
new_constraint: The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.
instruction: Write an email to my boss telling him that I am quitting. Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding jargon and ensuring clarity. The phrase "Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!" must appear at least once in the email.

Subject: Upcoming Change of Position

Dear [Manager's Name],

I am writing to formally confirm my decision to leave the company effective [Last Working Date]. Over the past few years, I have valued the experiences I have gained under your leadership.

Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!

This transition marks the next chapter in my career. I want to ensure a smooth handover before I depart. I am available to discuss training my replacement or wrapping up outstanding tasks over the coming week. Please feel free to schedule a brief conversation at your convenience.

Thank you for your support during my time here. I wish you and the team continued success.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>

final_answer:
Subject: Upcoming Change of Position

Dear [Manager's Name],

I am writing to formally confirm my decision to leave the company effective [Last Working Date]. Over the past few years, I have valued the experiences I have gained under your leadership.

Hey boss, guess what? I'm peacing out!

This transition marks the next chapter in my career. I want to ensure a smooth handover before I depart. I am available to discuss training my replacement or wrapping up outstanding tasks over the coming week. Please feel free to schedule a brief conversation at your convenience.

Thank you for your support during my time here. I wish you and the team continued success.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>
